name: HTTP Protocol Anomaly Detection Rules
objective: Detect malformed and non-compliant HTTP requests that commonly indicate evasion, parser confusion, request smuggling preparation, or reconnaissance against edge/application infrastructure.
risk_mitigated: Header injection via CRLF in header names/values
Oversized header fields and aggregate header abuse
HTTP protocol version and framing violations
Malformed request line and conflicting message semantics
description: Baseline protocol anomaly pack intended for translation into vendor-native WAF controls. Rules focus on request syntax validity, header safety, and strict HTTP parsing constraints to reduce evasion opportunities before application logic is reached.

rules.title: Header name/value CRLF injection
rules.description: Block requests where header names or values contain encoded or raw CR/LF characters that can enable header injection or response splitting paths.

rules.title: Abnormal HTTP method
rules.description: Allow only explicitly approved methods for the protected surface and block unknown, mixed-case abuse variants, or tunneling methods not used by the application.

rules.title: Oversized single header
rules.description: Detect and block single header field values beyond expected operational limits to mitigate buffer and parser stress attempts.

rules.title: Excessive aggregate header size
rules.description: Block requests with aggregate header bytes above platform tolerance to reduce oversized metadata abuse and intermediary desync edge cases.

rules.title: Conflicting transfer semantics
rules.description: Block conflicting Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding combinations and malformed chunked indicators often associated with request smuggling attempts.

rules.title: Invalid HTTP version or request line
rules.description: Detect malformed request line structure or unsupported protocol versions outside explicitly accepted values.

rules.title: Illegal header name characters
rules.description: Block header names containing characters outside token grammar to prevent parser ambiguity across intermediaries.

deployment_notes: Start in log/challenge mode for 7-14 days to baseline legitimate traffic.
Review legitimate method usage per path before enforcing strict method allowlist globally.
Tune header size thresholds to upstream limits (CDN, LB, ingress, app server) and pick the lowest safe bound.
If legacy clients exist, scope protocol-version checks by endpoint or client segment before full enforcement.
Enforce blocking first on internet-facing dynamic routes, then expand to all routes after validation.
tuning_notes: Exclude known large-cookie paths (e.g., SSO callbacks) from single-header and aggregate-header limits where justified.
Normalize method casing prior to evaluation if vendor engine supports transformations.
For APIs using uncommon methods (e.g., PROPFIND), add explicit per-service exceptions rather than broad global allowance.
Pair conflicting transfer-semantic rule with upstream proxy hardening to preserve parser consistency.

primary_signals: Rate of blocked protocol anomalies by rule id
Top offending source IPs/ASNs and user agents
Anomaly spikes by URI and edge POP/region
False-positive rate from trusted client segments
alert_recommendations: Alert on >3x baseline increase in any high-severity protocol anomaly over 15 minutes.
Alert when conflicting transfer semantic violations exceed threshold on a single route.
Create investigation ticket when repeated abnormal methods target privileged/admin paths.

references: OWASP Web Security Testing Guide - HTTP Request Smuggling
